summary: DHH announces that The Robot Co-op, founded by three ex-Amazon employees in Seattle, is hiring four technical positions and will be building their first project using Ruby on Rails. He frames this as part of a growing wave of commercial interest in the Rails platform, teasing future announcements about other companies adopting Rails for projects and consultancy work.
key_points: The Robot Co-op (founded by ex-Amazonians Josh, Daniel, and Erik) is hiring four technical roles to work with Ruby on Rails
This represents one of the earliest commercial adoptions of Ruby on Rails as a development platform
DHH signals that multiple companies are approaching him about launching Rails projects or doing Rails consultancy, suggesting growing commercial momentum
money_quotes: If you're interested in working commercially with Ruby on Rails alongside an awesome crew of clued-in people, this is stellar opportunity.
This is the first announcement from a series of commercial opportunities that's growing around the Ruby on Rails platform.
I've been in contact with a number of companies that are interested in launching projects of their own or doing consultancy work with the platform.
key_insight: The Robot Co-op's hiring marks an early milestone in Rails' transition from side project to commercially viable platform, with DHH positioning it as the beginning of a broader wave of adoption.
